mnemonics.title: W.O.R.S Parameters
mnemonics.content: Wait3 Parameters Order: Wait(Options, &Status, &Rusage) - Remember 'W.O.R.S' like a horse race where options come first, then status check, finally resource usage

mnemonics.title: Zombie Zapper
mnemonics.content: Z3RO Zombies: Wait3 Reaps Offspring (Zombie + 3 parameters). Remember it prevents 3 problems: Zombies, Resource leaks, Orphan status

mnemonics.title: Return Value Reminder
mnemonics.content: PID Brings Closure: Positive ID when child dies, -1 for errors, 0 with WNOHANG. Like process closure documents

mnemonics.title: Rusage Rainbow
mnemonics.content: CUTe MICE Resource Usage: CPU-times, User-time, System-time; Minor faults, Involuntary ctx switches, Children stats, Exit status

acronyms.usage: Non-blocking option: Parent doesn't hang if no child exited
Macro to check if child exited normally
Struct containing CPU time, memory, I/O stats
Wait3 prevents Zombies, Zaps Zombies, Zero lingering processes

visualTips.visualization: Imagine a parent process holding 3 balloons labeled Options, Status, and Rusage. Popping them in order releases child process info
Picture a tombstone (child process) with a zombie hand sticking out. Wait3 is the caretaker that buries it completely
Visualize a report card with sections: CPU Time (user/sys), Memory (page faults), I/O (blocks), Context Switches (voluntary/involuntary)
Imagine a waiting room with an exit sign. Parent can leave immediately if no child is ready (non-blocking)

analogies.analogy: Like a courier service tracker - you get notified when package (child) arrives, with delivery report (status) and shipping metrics (rusage)
Wait3 is like a funeral director - professionally handles deceased processes (zombies) and provides death certificates (exit codes)
Similar to a car's trip computer - shows total fuel used (CPU time), distance (memory), and maintenance alerts (signals)
Like checking your mailbox while cooking - peek quickly (non-blocking), return immediately if no mail, resume cooking
